channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: John Cena describes how his feelings about death have changed over time
item.description: Each week, a guest draws a card from NPR's Wild Card deck and answers a big question about their life. After 24 years in the WWE ring, John Cena retired from professional wrestling last year.
item.content:encoded: Each week, a guest draws a card from NPR's Wild Card deck and answers a big question about their life. After 24 years in the WWE ring, John Cena retired from professional wrestling last year.

item.title: Everyone expected more from the evening 'Invite,' audience included
item.description: Two couples spend an ill-advised evening together in the tense comedy The Invite. Olivia Wilde, Seth Rogen, Penélope Cruz and Edward Norton, star in the film, which plays it a little too safe.
item.content:encoded: Two couples spend an ill-advised evening together in the tense comedy The Invite. Olivia Wilde, Seth Rogen, Penélope Cruz and Edward Norton, star in the film, which plays it a little too safe.
item.dc:creator: Justin Chang

item.title: Remembering James Burrows, a prolific director of modern comedy
item.description: David Bianculli offers an appreciation, then we listen back to a '06 interview with Burrows, who died June 19. He directed over 1,000 sitcom episodes, co-created Cheers and chose the cast for Friends.
item.content:encoded: David Bianculli offers an appreciation, then we listen back to a '06 interview with Burrows, who died June 19. He directed over 1,000 sitcom episodes, co-created Cheers and chose the cast for Friends.
item.dc:creator: David Bianculli

item.title: Organizers say the Great American State Fair is for everyone. Some disagree
item.description: The 16-day state fair on the National Mall kicked off with a Trump rally, and not all 50 states are taking part. Organizers say there's nothing partisan about celebrating America's 250th birthday.
item.content:encoded:
The 16-day state fair on the National Mall kicked off with a Trump rally, and not all 50 states are taking part. Organizers say there's nothing partisan about celebrating America's 250th birthday.

(Image credit: Andrew Leyden)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Why Taylor Swift's rumored wedding has fans watching New York
item.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are rumored to celebrate at Madison Square Garden on July 3, with speculation ranging from a wedding reception to surprise musical performances.
item.content:encoded:
Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are rumored to celebrate at Madison Square Garden on July 3, with speculation ranging from a wedding reception to surprise musical performances.

(Image credit: Timothy A. Clary)
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: One bourbon maker is aging spirits on a barge in the Mississippi for a unique flavor
item.description: There's a new way to age Bourbon in Kentucky. In barges on the Mississippi, which some say gives it a distinct flavor.
item.content:encoded: There's a new way to age Bourbon in Kentucky. In barges on the Mississippi, which some say gives it a distinct flavor.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Saad

item.title: 'The Bear' is back in the kitchen
item.description: In some ways, the fifth and final season of The Bear feels less daring — but after four seasons, the small wins mean more.
item.content:encoded:
In some ways, the fifth and final season of The Bear feels less daring — but after four seasons, the small wins mean more.

(Image credit: FX)
item.dc:creator: Linda Holmes

item.title: Playwright Anna Deavere Smith tells her own family story in 'Basil Biggs'
item.description: Smith's new show is about her great-great-grandfather, a free Black man who reburied the Union dead at Gettysburg and prepared the ground for Lincoln's most famous speech.
item.content:encoded: Smith's new show is about her great-great-grandfather, a free Black man who reburied the Union dead at Gettysburg and prepared the ground for Lincoln's most famous speech.
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley

item.title: The Obamas team up with Larry David in this irreverent look at American history
item.description: Each sketch of HBO's seven-part comedy series, Life, Larry and the Pursuit of Unhappiness: An Almost History of America, starts with historical fact — then veers wildly, and enjoyably, off the rails.
item.content:encoded: Each sketch of HBO's seven-part comedy series, Life, Larry and the Pursuit of Unhappiness: An Almost History of America, starts with historical fact — then veers wildly, and enjoyably, off the rails.
item.dc:creator: David Bianculli

item.title: Norman Rockwell art that ignited a lawsuit and a love story is now on public view
item.description: So You Want to See The President! depicts a procession of visitors waiting to see Franklin Delano Roosevelt. The original 1943 Rockwell suite of illustrations goes on public view Thursday in D.C.
item.content:encoded:
So You Want to See the President! Click to enlarge.'/>

So You Want to See The President! depicts a procession of visitors waiting to see Franklin Delano Roosevelt. The original 1943 Rockwell suite of illustrations goes on public view Thursday in D.C.

(Image credit: Bruce M. White)
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Blair
